sections.paragraph:
ἀμέριστος, ον (since Pla., Tim. 35a; PRyl 585, 3; 10 [II b.c.] also Philo; Ar. [Milne 74, 11]; Ath. 18, 2 mostly=‘indivisible’) undivided (SIG 783, 35f. of a married couple: παρʼ ἀμφοτέροις ἀμέριστος ὁμόνοια) ἀγαπᾶν ἐν ἀ. καρδίᾳ to love w. undivided heart ITr 13:2; cp. IPhld 6:2.—DELG s.v. μείρομαι.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 53
